clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.
The Service Provider shall perform the corporate photography services described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] at [EVENT_LOCATION] on [EVENT_DATE].
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [EVENT_DATE] at [EVENT_LOCATION], subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.

Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Client receives a [LICENSE_SCOPE] license to use the Deliverables for [PERMITTED_USE], including advertising and marketing use consistent with that scope. The Service Provider retains copyright in the Deliverables.

Where the Client requires use of the Deliverables beyond [PERMITTED_USE], the Parties shall agree an additional license fee of [EXTENDED_LICENSE_FEE] for the extended scope of [EXTENDED_PERMITTED_USE].

The Service Provider shall keep confidential any non-public business information observed or disclosed while performing the Services at [EVENT_LOCATION], and shall not disclose it without the Client's prior written consent.
The Client shall ensure the Service Provider has timely access to [EVENT_LOCATION] and any information reasonably necessary to perform the Services.
The Service Provider shall perform the Services with the skill and care ordinarily exercised by professionals providing comparable services under comparable circumstances.

The Client shall be responsible for ensuring [EVENT_LOCATION] is reasonably safe for the Service Provider to perform the Services. The Service Provider shall not be liable for delay or inability to perform caused by unsafe site conditions at [EVENT_LOCATION].
Each Party's total liability arising from this Agreement shall not exceed [TOTAL_VALUE], except for loss arising from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud.
If the Client cancels the Services more than [CANCELLATION_NOTICE_PERIOD_DAYS] days before [EVENT_DATE], the Client shall forfeit [CANCELLATION_FEE_PERCENT] of [TOTAL_VALUE].

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — Commercial Usage Rights and Extended Licensing kept structurally distinct so scope-creep disputes are prevented at the definition layer. Duplication: none between Commercial Usage Rights (base grant) and Extended Licensing (add-on grant). Loophole closed: Liability site-conditions clause added to protect photographer from unsafe premises risk absent from Wedding variant, since corporate shoots occur on Client's own property. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: Confidentiality upgraded to Required (vs Optional in Wedding) given heightened exposure to business-sensitive environments. Complexity: kept minimal, reusing Wedding pattern where applicable per consistency rule. Enforceability: explicit fee tied to extended use, not open-ended. Attack: Client uses images in a national ad campaign under a license scoped only for internal use — mitigated: Commercial Usage Rights ties usage strictly to [PERMITTED_USE], with Extended Licensing required for broader use. Attack: Photographer injured due to unsafe office conditions and blames delay on the photographer — mitigated by Liability Site Conditions Clause. Attack: Photographer discloses observed confidential business plans — mitigated by upgraded Required Confidentiality Block. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
